routines.title: Digest Ligate Extract (Sticky)
routines.summary: Digest one sequence with two enzymes, ligate selected fragments using sticky-end semantics, and extract one output region with restriction-family preflight checks.
routines.purpose: Assemble or subclone by deterministic restriction-site cuts followed by sticky-end ligation and optional region extraction.
routines.mechanism: Site-specific endonuclease digest defines fragment boundaries; ligation joins compatible sticky ends.
requires: Resolvable enzyme names with expected recognition sites in the selected sequence.
Compatible sticky ends for intended ligation products.
contraindications: Fragments or enzymes that cannot produce compatible sticky termini for the intended assembly.
disambiguation_questions: Do you want junctions dictated by restriction sites, or by designed overlaps/overhang tokens?
Is compatible sticky-end ligation acceptable for all intended joins?
failure_modes: Unknown enzyme name or missing recognition site in the input sequence.
Invalid fragment indices or extract bounds after digest/ligation.

input_ports.description: 1-based digest fragment index for ligation left input.
1-based digest fragment index for ligation right input.
Extract start coordinate from first ligation product.
Extract end coordinate from first ligation product.

output_ports.description: Extracted ligation product output sequence ID.

routines.title: Gibson Two-Fragment Overlap Preview
routines.summary: Validate two-fragment overlap compatibility with Gibson-specific preflight checks and generate deterministic forward-order preview products.
routines.purpose: Plan overlap-driven two-fragment assembly without relying on restriction enzyme sites.
routines.mechanism: Adjacent fragment suffix/prefix homology is validated, then deterministic forward-order preview output is generated.
requires: Two input fragments with sufficient homologous overlap at the intended junction.
contraindications: Short or mismatched overlap region between adjacent fragments.
disambiguation_questions: Are overlaps already designed and biologically acceptable for this junction?
Do you need Type IIS overhang grammar instead of overlap homology?
failure_modes: Overlap mismatch between adjacent fragments for configured overlap length.

input_ports.description: Left fragment sequence ID in intended assembly order.
Right fragment sequence ID in intended assembly order.
Homology overlap length used for Gibson preflight validation.

routines.title: Branch Reverse Complement
routines.summary: Create a branch copy of a sequence and reverse-complement that copy.

input_ports.description: Input sequence for branch + reverse-complement derivation.

routines.title: PCR Site Insertion Then Digest
routines.summary: Amplify with advanced primer configuration to introduce a site and digest the amplicon.

routines.title: gRNA Candidate Priority Scan
routines.summary: Generate, score, and rank guide candidates for a target region.

routines.title: gRNA Anchor Window Scan
routines.summary: Generate guide candidates between two anchors and score the resulting set.

routines.title: gRNA Practical Filter and Oligos
routines.summary: Filter guide candidates with practical constraints and generate cloning oligos.

routines.title: Golden Gate Type IIS Single Insert
routines.summary: Digest vector+insert with one Type IIS enzyme and generate sticky-end ligation preview output.
routines.purpose: Assemble one insert into one destination vector using programmable Type IIS overhangs.
routines.mechanism: Type IIS digest-ligation cycling enforces user-defined overhang junction grammar.
requires: Type IIS-capable enzyme resolved in the enzyme catalog.
Explicit non-conflicting overhang plan at intended junctions.
contraindications: Conflicting or duplicated non-terminal overhang tokens.
disambiguation_questions: Do you need explicit short overhang token control at each junction?
Should assembly avoid homology-overlap style junctions?
failure_modes: Unknown or non-Type-IIS enzyme selection.
Missing required recognition site or invalid overhang token set.

input_ports.description: Golden Gate destination vector sequence ID.
Insert sequence ID for single-fragment insertion.

output_ports.description: Deterministic Golden Gate output sequence ID.

routines.title: Golden Gate Type IIS Multi Insert
routines.summary: Golden Gate baseline for vector + two inserts with explicit junction overhang preflight.
routines.purpose: Assemble vector plus multiple inserts in one Type IIS-oriented routine with explicit junction overhang choreography.
routines.mechanism: Type IIS digest-ligation with ordered multi-junction overhang validation before execution.
requires: Type IIS-capable enzyme and complete junction-overhang token list.
contraindications: Repeated non-terminal junction-overhang tokens across distinct joins.
disambiguation_questions: Are all intended joins representable by one ordered overhang-token chain?
Would overlap-based multi-fragment assembly better fit available fragments?
failure_modes: Overhang-token count does not match expected junction count.
Non-unique internal overhang token detected.

input_ports.description: Golden Gate destination vector sequence ID.
Comma-separated expected junction overhang token list.

output_ports.description: Deterministic Golden Gate multi-insert output sequence ID.

routines.title: Golden Gate Type IIS Destination Cleanup
routines.summary: Digest destination backbone and keep one deterministic fragment/extract for downstream assembly.
routines.purpose: Prepare a destination backbone fragment deterministically before downstream assembly.
routines.mechanism: Type IIS digest produces candidate fragments and deterministic keep/extract selection.
requires: Destination sequence and Type IIS enzyme with at least one valid cut site.
contraindications: No digest fragment matching the intended keep/extract bounds.
disambiguation_questions: Is this step only for destination cleanup, not full insert assembly?
failure_modes: Requested keep_fragment is out of digest fragment range.

routines.title: Gateway BP Single Insert
routines.summary: Gateway BP planning baseline with att-site token preflight.
routines.purpose: Model Gateway BP recombination step from attB/attP inputs into entry-clone style outputs.
routines.mechanism: Phase-aware att-site token validation gates BP-step execution.
requires: Gateway phase token set to BP with attB/attP-compatible token set.
contraindications: Mixed BP/LR token sets or incompatible att-site phase selection.
disambiguation_questions: Are you performing BP entry reaction now, or LR destination reaction?
failure_modes: Gateway phase/site mismatch for BP validation.

routines.title: Gateway LR Single Insert
routines.summary: Gateway LR planning baseline with att-site token preflight.
routines.purpose: Model Gateway LR recombination step from entry clone to destination vector outputs.
routines.mechanism: Phase-aware att-site token validation gates LR-step execution.
requires: Gateway phase token set to LR with attL/attR-compatible token set.
contraindications: Mixed BP/LR token sets or incompatible att-site phase selection.
disambiguation_questions: Is the entry clone already available and ready for LR destination transfer?
failure_modes: Gateway phase/site mismatch for LR validation.

routines.title: Gateway BP+LR Two-Step
routines.summary: Gateway two-step BP then LR baseline with phase-aware token preflight.
routines.purpose: Execute Gateway planning as an explicit BP then LR staged workflow.
routines.mechanism: Per-step att-site token validation is applied for BP and LR phases in sequence.
requires: Inputs for both BP and LR phases with phase-appropriate att tokens.
contraindications: Only one phase is available but two-step workflow is selected.
disambiguation_questions: Do you need both Gateway phases now, or only BP or LR?
failure_modes: Phase-token mismatch in one of the two Gateway stages.

routines.title: TOPO TA Single Insert
routines.summary: TOPO TA baseline with terminal-base compatibility preflight.
routines.purpose: Perform TOPO TA-style single-insert cloning with terminal A/T compatibility checks.
routines.mechanism: Mode-aware end-compatibility preflight validates TA termini before assembly preview.
requires: Insert/vector termini compatible with TA mode.
contraindications: Terminal chemistry incompatible with TA mode.
disambiguation_questions: Do insert/vector ends require TA, blunt, or directional CACC mode?
failure_modes: Terminal-base mismatch for TA mode.

routines.title: TOPO Blunt Single Insert
routines.summary: TOPO blunt baseline with mode-aware end-compatibility preflight.
routines.purpose: Perform TOPO blunt-end single-insert cloning with blunt-end compatibility checks.
routines.mechanism: Mode-aware end-compatibility preflight validates blunt termini before assembly preview.
requires: Insert/vector termini compatible with blunt mode.
contraindications: Terminal chemistry incompatible with blunt mode.
disambiguation_questions: Are fragment termini blunt or do they require TA/CACC-specific chemistry?
failure_modes: Terminal-base mismatch for blunt mode.

routines.title: TOPO Directional CACC Single Insert
routines.summary: TOPO directional baseline with CACC-prefixed insert preflight.
routines.purpose: Perform directional TOPO cloning with CACC-prefixed orientation constraints.
routines.mechanism: Mode-aware preflight validates directional CACC insert constraint before assembly preview.
requires: Insert representation compatible with directional CACC mode.
contraindications: No directional CACC-compatible insert context for intended orientation.
disambiguation_questions: Is strict orientation control required at cloning entry?
failure_modes: Directional CACC mode selected without compatible insert context.

routines.title: TA Cloning Single Insert
routines.summary: TA cloning baseline with terminal-base compatibility preflight.
routines.purpose: Perform TA cloning with explicit A/T terminal compatibility checks.
routines.mechanism: Terminal-base compatibility preflight validates TA overhang pairing before assembly preview.
requires: A/T-compatible terminal-base pairing between vector and insert.
contraindications: Terminal-base pairing incompatible with TA overhang model.
disambiguation_questions: Are termini A/T-compatible or G/C-compatible for this insertion?
failure_modes: Terminal-base mismatch for TA mode.

routines.title: GC Cloning Single Insert
routines.summary: GC cloning baseline with terminal-base compatibility preflight.
routines.purpose: Perform GC cloning with explicit G/C terminal compatibility checks.
routines.mechanism: Terminal-base compatibility preflight validates GC overhang pairing before assembly preview.
requires: G/C-compatible terminal-base pairing between vector and insert.
contraindications: Terminal-base pairing incompatible with GC overhang model.
disambiguation_questions: Are termini G/C-compatible rather than A/T-compatible?
failure_modes: Terminal-base mismatch for GC mode.

routines.title: In-Fusion Two-Fragment Overlap
routines.summary: In-Fusion two-fragment overlap planning baseline with adjacent overlap preflight.
routines.purpose: Plan two-fragment overlap assembly with In-Fusion-family overlap checks.
routines.mechanism: Adjacent overlap-chain preflight validates suffix/prefix overlap compatibility.
requires: Two fragments with sufficient homologous overlap at the intended junction.
contraindications: Insufficient or mismatched overlap between adjacent fragments.
disambiguation_questions: Is In-Fusion-specific overlap workflow required, or is Gibson/NEBuilder acceptable?
failure_modes: Overlap mismatch for configured overlap length.

routines.title: In-Fusion Multi-Fragment Overlap
routines.summary: In-Fusion three-fragment overlap baseline with adjacent overlap preflight.
routines.purpose: Plan multi-fragment overlap assembly with In-Fusion-family overlap checks.
routines.mechanism: Adjacent overlap-chain preflight validates each successive overlap junction.
requires: All adjacent fragments have sufficient overlap in intended order.
contraindications: One or more adjacent overlap junctions are incompatible.
disambiguation_questions: Do you prefer overlap-driven multi-fragment assembly over overhang-token choreography?
failure_modes: At least one overlap junction fails adjacent homology check.

input_ports.description: Overlap length for adjacent preflight checks.

routines.title: NEBuilder HiFi Two-Fragment Overlap
routines.summary: NEBuilder HiFi two-fragment overlap planning baseline with overlap-chain preflight.
routines.purpose: Plan two-fragment overlap assembly with NEBuilder HiFi-family overlap checks.
routines.mechanism: Adjacent overlap-chain preflight validates suffix/prefix overlap compatibility.
requires: Two fragments with sufficient homologous overlap at the intended junction.
contraindications: Insufficient or mismatched overlap between adjacent fragments.
disambiguation_questions: Is NEBuilder HiFi workflow required, or is Gibson/In-Fusion acceptable?
failure_modes: Overlap mismatch for configured overlap length.

routines.title: NEBuilder HiFi Multi-Fragment Overlap
routines.summary: NEBuilder HiFi three-fragment overlap baseline with adjacent overlap preflight.
routines.purpose: Plan multi-fragment overlap assembly with NEBuilder HiFi-family overlap checks.
routines.mechanism: Adjacent overlap-chain preflight validates each successive overlap junction.
requires: All adjacent fragments have sufficient overlap in intended order.
contraindications: One or more adjacent overlap junctions are incompatible.
disambiguation_questions: Do you need NEBuilder HiFi for this multi-fragment overlap design?
failure_modes: At least one overlap junction fails adjacent homology check.

input_ports.description: Overlap length for adjacent preflight checks.
